connection.offlineHint: WebSocket disconnected — using periodic polling. Reconnecting…

generate.noModelDownloaded: No model downloaded yet. Go to Models to download one.
generate.promptPlaceholder: A serene mountain lake at sunrise, ultra detailed
generate.samplerHint: FLUX and SD 3.x always use their native sampler.
generate.previewAlt: Live preview of the image being generated

reference.title: Reference image (optional)
reference.styleUnsupported: Style needs an SD 1.5 or SDXL model.

info.title: Optional: condition generation on an image — make variations of it (img2img) or borrow its style (IP-Adapter).
info.mode: Variations (img2img) repaints your image guided by the prompt. Style (IP-Adapter) keeps your prompt's composition but adopts the reference's style/subject.
info.strength: How far the result may drift from the reference. Low (~0.3) stays close to it; high (~0.8) changes more; near 1.0 almost ignores it. Actual steps ≈ steps × strength.
info.styleStrength: How strongly the reference image's style influences the result. Higher = stronger style transfer.

info.model: The downloaded model used to generate. Different models (SD 1.5, SDXL, FLUX…) have different quality, speed and VRAM needs.
info.prompt: Describe what you want to see — subject, style, lighting, composition. More specific usually works better.
info.negativePrompt: Describe what to avoid (e.g. blurry, extra fingers, watermark, text). Optional.
info.sampler: The denoising algorithm. DPM++ 2M Karras is a solid all-round default; samplers trade off speed, detail and how quickly they converge.
info.steps: Number of denoising steps. More = more detail and time, with diminishing returns. 20–40 is usually plenty.
info.guidance: Guidance scale (CFG): how strictly the image follows the prompt. Low = more creative/loose; high = closer to the prompt but can look harsh. Typically 5–9.
info.batch: How many images to generate in one run. Each uses an incrementing seed, so you get variations to pick from.
info.size: Output size in pixels. Use the model's native size for best results (512×512 for SD 1.5, 1024×1024 for SDXL). Larger costs more VRAM/time.
info.seed: Starting randomness. The same seed + settings reproduces the same image. Leave empty for a new random seed each run.
info.livePreview: Show a rough preview after each step while generating. Slightly slower; SD 1.5 / SDXL only.

generate.empty: Your generated image will appear here

snippets.saveHint: Save the current field text as a reusable snippet.
snippets.name: Snippet name

gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.empty: No images yet. Generate one to see it here.

prompt.label: Upscaler prompt (optional)
prompt.help: Guides the diffusion upscaler toward more detail. Leave empty for a faithful enlargement.

tiling.label: Tiled upscaling
tiling.help: Splits large images into tiles and stitches the result — bounds VRAM and speeds up inference. Auto only tiles when the image exceeds the tile size.

format.title: Target format
format.help: Reframe the result to a target aspect ratio. The strategy decides how the extra area is handled.

reframe.coverHelp: Crop to the target ratio — loses the overflowing edges.
reframe.containHelp: Fit the image and fill the rest with a blurred backdrop.
reframe.edgeHelp: Extend the border pixels to fill the new area.
reframe.outpaintHelp: Generate new content in the added area with an inpaint model. Tip: describe the background/scene, not the subject.

outpaint.needsModel: Outpainting needs an inpaint model (≈ {size} GB).
outpaint.modelHelp: The inpaint model used to generate the new area. Manage models on the Models page.
outpaint.promptHelp: Describes the scene generated in the added area. Tip: describe the background/scene, not the subject.

result.title: Result
result.empty: Your upscaled image will appear here.

picker.title: Choose an image from the gallery
picker.empty: No images in the gallery yet.

models.title: Model catalog
models.gatedHint: Gated — add a HuggingFace token in Settings
models.fitTooltipGpu: Runs fully on the GPU (~{vram} GB of {total} GB VRAM).
models.fitTooltipOffload: Needs ~{vram} GB VRAM but the GPU has {total} GB — will run slower via CPU offloading to system RAM.
models.fitTooltipTooLarge: Needs ~{vram} GB VRAM; does not fit even with CPU offloading.
models.fitTooltipCpu: No GPU detected — runs on the CPU (slow).

browser.title: Add a model from HuggingFace
browser.searchHint: Search diffusers text-to-image models, or paste a full repo id (owner/name).
browser.incompatible: Not a diffusers-format model — cannot be loaded here.
browser.added: Download started — see the models list.

settings.title: Settings
settings.hfTokenHelp: Optional. Speeds up downloads and enables gated models.

performance.title: Performance
performance.vaeTilingHelp: Decode the image in tiles — lower VRAM at high resolution (recommended, esp. for upscaling).
performance.vaeSlicingHelp: Decode images one at a time — saves memory on batches.
performance.xformersHelp: Faster, memory-efficient attention (only if xformers is installed; otherwise ignored).
performance.note: Applies to generation and upscaling. Takes effect on the next run.

engines.title: Upscale & outpaint models
engines.subtitle: Engines used for AI upscaling and outpainting. Add custom Real-ESRGAN weights, SD x4 upscalers or inpaint models.

browser.title: Add an upscale / outpaint engine
browser.repoIdHint: owner/name — e.g. a Real-ESRGAN weights repo or a diffusers upscaler/inpaint repo.
browser.incompatible: No usable weights found in this repo for the selected engine type.
